title: Profiling a dataset with dataProfilerR
text: show profiler surfaces, small frame missing values, outlier, constant column, high-cardinality text column.
code: set.seed(1) n <- 200 df <- data.frame(   age        = round(rnorm(n, 40, 12)),   income     = c(rlnorm(n - 1, log(50000), 0.4), 5e6),   # one extreme outlier   signup     = as.Date("2025-01-01") + sample(0:600, n, replace = TRUE),   plan       = sample(c("free", "pro", "enterprise"), n, replace = TRUE),   region     = sample(c("NA", "EU", "APAC"), n, replace = TRUE),   constant   = 1L,                                        # zero-variance column   note       = replicate(n, paste(sample(letters, 12), collapse = "")),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) df$income[sample(n, 20)] <- NA          # inject missingness df$plan[sample(n, 8)]    <- NA

title: Profiling a dataset with dataProfilerR
text: print() gives headline: dimensions, type breakdown, missingness, quality score. Note score 100 – missingness constant column cost points.
code: p <- profile_data(df, dataset_name = "customers") #> Warning in stats::cor(num_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = m): the #> standard deviation is zero #> Warning in stats::cor(num_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = m): the #> standard deviation is zero #> Warning in stats::cor(num_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = m): the #> standard deviation is zero #> Warning in stats::cor(num_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = m): the #> standard deviation is zero p #> <data_profile> #>   dataset : customers #>   size    : 200 rows x 7 columns #>   types   : categorical=2, date=1, integer=1, numeric=2, text=1 #>   missing : 2.0% of cells; 86.5% of rows complete #>   quality : 96.2 / 100 (grade A) #>   most missing: income (10.0%), plan (4.0%) #>   use summary() for details and plot() for figures.

title: Profiling a dataset with dataProfilerR
text: numeric summary shows income heavily right-skewed (large positive skewness kurtosis) thanks injected outlier, outlier table flags . age looks roughly symmetric.
code: summary(p) #> Data profile for 'customers' (200 x 7), quality 96.2 (A) #>  #> -- numeric summary -- #>    column   n n_missing      mean        sd     variance      min       q1 #>       age 200         0    40.385     11.15 1.243180e+02    13.00    33.00 #>    income 180        20 82158.549 369253.45 1.363481e+11 15743.93 40392.28 #>  constant 200         0     1.000      0.00 0.000000e+00     1.00     1.00 #>    median       q3     max      iqr skewness kurtosis #>     39.00    47.00      69    14.00    0.190   -0.199 #>  50065.35 67074.75 5000000 26682.47   13.233  173.753 #>      1.00     1.00       1     0.00       NA       NA #>  #> -- columns with missing values -- #>  column n_missing pct_missing #>  income        20          10 #>    plan         8           4 #>  #> -- normality (Shapiro-Wilk) -- #>    column n_used shapiro_p normal #>       age    200  3.93e-01   TRUE #>    income    180  9.16e-29  FALSE #>  constant    200        NA  FALSE #>  #> -- outliers (iqr) -- #>    column n_outliers  pct #>       age          1 0.50 #>    income          4 2.22 #>  constant          0 0.00 #>  #> -- strongest correlations (pearson) -- #>    var1     var2 correlation #>     age   income      -0.035 #>     age constant          NA #>  income constant          NA #>  #> -- date columns -- #>  column   n n_missing        min        max range_days n_unique max_gap_days #>  signup 200         0 2025-01-03 2026-08-24        598      167           16 #>  #> -- categorical association (Cramer's V) -- #>        plan region #> plan   1.00   0.02 #> region 0.02   1.00

what: The object is just a list
title: Profiling a dataset with dataProfilerR
text: Everything accessible directly, makes profile easy use programmatically:
code: p$metadata$column_types #>           age        income        signup          plan        region  #>     "numeric"     "numeric"        "date" "categorical" "categorical"  #>      constant          note  #>     "integer"        "text" p$diagnostics$quality$components #> completeness   uniqueness  variability  cleanliness  #>         98.0        100.0         85.7         99.1 head(p$statistics$numeric[, c("column", "mean", "sd", "skewness")]) #>     column      mean           sd   skewness #> 1      age    40.385     11.14981  0.1904266 #> 2   income 82158.549 369253.44951 13.2334718 #> 3 constant     1.000      0.00000         NA

title: Profiling a dataset with dataProfilerR
text: figures built profile_data() retrieved plot().    can also call plotting functions directly without full profile, e.g. plot_boxplots(df) plot_pairs(df, c("age", "income")).
code: plot(p, which = "missing") plot(p, which = "distribution", column = "income") plot(p, which = "correlation")

title: Profiling a dataset with dataProfilerR
text: build_plots = FALSE skips figure construction wide data. outlier_method can "iqr" (default), "zscore", "robust" (median/MAD). cor_method accepts "pearson", "spearman", . normality = FALSE skips Shapiro-Wilk / Anderson-Darling tests.
code: p2 <- profile_data(df, build_plots = FALSE, outlier_method = "robust",                    cor_method = "spearman") #> Warning in stats::cor(num_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = m): the #> standard deviation is zero #> Warning in stats::cor(num_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = m): the #> standard deviation is zero #> Warning in stats::cor(num_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = m): the #> standard deviation is zero p2$diagnostics$outliers$per_column #>     column n_outliers  pct #> 1      age          0 0.00 #> 2   income          3 1.67 #> 3 constant          0 0.00

title: Profiling a dataset with dataProfilerR
text: Categorical columns get association matrix (Cramer’s V):  Date columns profiled range gaps: can compare numeric columns across levels factor:
code: p$statistics$association #>              plan     region #> plan   1.00000000 0.02227124 #> region 0.02227124 1.00000000 plot(p, which = "association") p$diagnostics$dates #>   column   n n_missing        min        max range_days n_unique max_gap_days #> 1 signup 200         0 2025-01-03 2026-08-24        598      167           16 pg <- profile_data(df, group_by = "plan") #> Warning in stats::cor(num_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = m): the #> standard deviation is zero #> Warning in stats::cor(num_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = m): the #> standard deviation is zero #> Warning in stats::cor(num_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = m): the #> standard deviation is zero #> Warning in stats::cor(num_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = m): the #> standard deviation is zero head(pg$diagnostics$groups$numeric_by_group, 8) #>        group   column  n n_missing         mean           sd   median #> 1 enterprise      age 70         0     40.87143     11.82103    40.50 #> 2 enterprise   income 63         7  53494.38196  20892.25942 49893.23 #> 3 enterprise constant 70         0      1.00000      0.00000     1.00 #> 4       free      age 59         0     40.16949     11.88907    40.00 #> 5       free   income 54         5  56576.18404  21851.55117 52620.50 #> 6       free constant 59         0      1.00000      0.00000     1.00 #> 7        pro      age 63         0     40.12698     10.23985    39.00 #> 8        pro   income 56         7 140692.97488 661501.68118 47833.91

title: Profiling a dataset with dataProfilerR
text: report() renders everything – tables figures – one self-contained HTML file. needs pandoc (usual R Markdown dependency).

title: Authors and Citation
text: Muhammad Farooqi. Author, maintainer.

text: Farooqi M (2026). dataProfilerR: Automated Exploratory Data Analysis Dataset Profiling. R package version 0.2.1, https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=dataProfilerR.
code: @Manual{,   title = {{dataProfilerR}: Automated Exploratory Data Analysis and Dataset Profiling},   author = {Muhammad Farooqi},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.1},   url = {https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=dataProfilerR}, }

what: Automated Exploratory Data Analysis and Dataset Profiling
title: Automated Exploratory Data Analysis and Dataset Profiling
text: Automated exploratory data analysis R. Point data frame returns structured profile — column types, missingness, distributional statistics, normality tests, outliers, correlations, data-quality score, ggplot2 figures — single function, profile_data(). aim cover first hour EDA ’d otherwise write hand every new dataset, keeping result plain, inspectable object can build .

title: Automated Exploratory Data Analysis and Dataset Profiling
text: Depends ggplot2. Anderson–Darling normality test additionally uses suggested nortest package; isn’t installed, Shapiro–Wilk run.

title: Automated Exploratory Data Analysis and Dataset Profiling
text: See vignette (vignette("dataProfilerR")) full walkthrough messy dataset.
code: library(dataProfilerR)  p <- profile_data(iris) p                                  # concise overview + quality score summary(p)                         # numeric summary, missingness, normality, outliers, correlations plot(p, which = "correlation")     # retrieve a figure plot(p, which = "distribution", column = "Sepal.Length")  # components are just list elements p$metadata$column_types p$diagnostics$quality$score p$statistics$numeric  # grouped comparison + a self-contained HTML report (needs pandoc) p <- profile_data(iris, group_by = "Species") p$diagnostics$groups$numeric_by_group report(p, "iris_report.html")

title: Automated Exploratory Data Analysis and Dataset Profiling
text: package organised pipeline independent, individually-callable functions, one orchestrator top: Design choices worth calling : S3, S4. profiling result data, behaviour. Modelling plain list class keeps transparent (str(p) just works), serialisable, easy extend new elements without redefining formal class. S4’s validity dispatch machinery overhead payoff . methods provided print, summary, plot. stage stands alone. infer_column_types(), detect_outliers(), plot_correlation() etc. work directly data frame vector, package useful piecemeal, orchestrator. Type inference drives rest. Columns classified (numeric/integer/date/logical/categorical/text) classification routes statistics plots apply. Fail early bad input. shared validator rejects non-data-frames, empty frames, duplicate/blank column names clear messages rather letting surface cryptic downstream errors. Minimal dependencies. ggplot2 beyond base/recommended packages. Skewness kurtosis implemented directly rather pulling moments; Anderson–Darling degrades gracefully nortest absent.
code: profile_data()                 <- orchestrator         ┌───────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┐    profiling            statistics                      visualization    ─────────            ──────────                       ────────────    infer_column_types   normality_tests                 plot_missing    analyze_missing      detect_outliers / outlier_summary plot_distribution    summarize_columns    correlation_analysis            plot_correlation    data_quality_score                                   plot_boxplots                                                         plot_pairs                           │                           ▼                   data_profile (S3 object)  ──  print() / summary() / plot()

title: Automated Exploratory Data Analysis and Dataset Profiling
text: Profiling Statistics Visualization (ggplot2) Pipeline, reporting & object

title: Automated Exploratory Data Analysis and Dataset Profiling
text: profile_data() returns S3 list four parts plus call: metadata — dataset name, dimensions, per-column types, type counts, timestamp. statistics — numeric summary, categorical summary, correlation matrices, categorical association matrix. diagnostics — missingness, normality, outliers, date-column profile, grouped comparison (group_by set), quality score. plots — ggplot2 objects (empty build_plots = FALSE; per-column distribution plots also skipped distributions = FALSE).

title: Automated Exploratory Data Analysis and Dataset Profiling
code: dataProfilerR/ ├── DESCRIPTION ├── NAMESPACE                # generated by roxygen2 ├── LICENSE ├── NEWS.md ├── R/ │   ├── dataProfilerR-package.R │   ├── utils.R              # validation + skewness/kurtosis │   ├── profiling.R          # types, missingness, summaries, quality score │   ├── statistics.R         # normality, outliers, correlation │   ├── association.R        # Cramer's V for categoricals │   ├── dates.R              # date/datetime profiling │   ├── groups.R             # grouped comparison │   ├── visualization.R      # ggplot2 functions │   ├── report.R             # HTML report (rmarkdown) │   ├── profile_data.R       # orchestrator + S3 constructor │   └── methods.R            # print / summary / plot ├── man/                     # generated by roxygen2 ├── tests/testthat/          # unit + edge-case tests └── vignettes/dataProfilerR.Rmd

title: Automated Exploratory Data Analysis and Dataset Profiling
text: testthat (edition 3) covers function plus edge cases — empty frames, wrong types, -NA columns, single-column frames, missing-column plot requests, output-shape consistency. Run devtools::test().

title: Automated Exploratory Data Analysis and Dataset Profiling
text: Added 0.2.0: report() (HTML), categorical_association() (Cramer’s V), analyze_dates(), compare_groups(), distributions = FALSE switch avoid eager per-column plots wide data. See NEWS.md. Still open / honest gaps: Numeric-vs-categorical effect sizes (e.g. eta-squared, group-mean differences tests) aren’t yet; compare_groups() reports descriptive summaries , significance. Date analysis shallow — range gaps, seasonality/trend. Distribution plots still eager unless opt distributions = FALSE; fully lazy, build--demand path cleaner. report() requires pandoc (usual R Markdown dependency); pandoc-free fallback. Text columns detected analysed beyond cardinality.

title: Automated Exploratory Data Analysis and Dataset Profiling
text: MIT © Muhammad Farooqi

title: Profile date / datetime columns — analyze_dates
text: Date/POSIXct column, reports count, missingness, range, largest gap consecutive (sorted, unique) timestamps – quick way spot coverage holes time series.

title: Profile date / datetime columns — analyze_dates
text: df data frame. types Optional named character vector column types; computed supplied.

title: Profile date / datetime columns — analyze_dates
text: data frame one row per date column (column, n, n_missing, min, max, range_days, n_unique, max_gap_days), NULL date columns.

title: Profile date / datetime columns — analyze_dates
code: df <- data.frame(d = as.Date("2026-01-01") + c(0, 1, 2, 10)) analyze_dates(df) #>   column n n_missing        min        max range_days n_unique max_gap_days #> 1      d 4         0 2026-01-01 2026-01-11         10        4            8

title: Analyse missing values — analyze_missing
text: Reports missingness per column overall, including many rows fully complete. NA counted missing (blank strings ).

title: Analyse missing values — analyze_missing
text: df data frame.

title: Analyse missing values — analyze_missing
text: list per_column (data frame column, n_missing, pct_missing) overall (list total/missing cell counts, pct_missing, complete_rows pct_complete_rows).

title: Analyse missing values — analyze_missing
code: analyze_missing(data.frame(a = c(1, NA, 3), b = c("x", "y", NA))) #> $per_column #>   column n_missing pct_missing #> 1      a         1       33.33 #> 2      b         1       33.33 #>  #> $overall #> $overall$total_cells #> [1] 6 #>  #> $overall$missing_cells #> [1] 2 #>  #> $overall$pct_missing #> [1] 33.33 #>  #> $overall$complete_rows #> [1] 1 #>  #> $overall$pct_complete_rows #> [1] 33.33 #>  #>

title: Categorical association (Cramer's V) — categorical_association
text: Computes Cramer's V every pair categorical/logical columns. V ranges 0 (association) 1 (perfect association) categorical analogue correlation matrix. derived chi-squared statistic: V = sqrt(X^2 / (n * (k - 1))), k smaller two factors' level counts.

title: Categorical association (Cramer's V) — categorical_association
text: df data frame. types Optional named character vector column types (infer_column_types()); computed supplied. max_levels Categorical columns many levels skipped (high-cardinality column makes chi-squared test unreliable table huge). Default 50.

title: Categorical association (Cramer's V) — categorical_association
text: symmetric numeric matrix Cramer's V unit diagonal, NULL fewer two eligible categorical columns present.

title: Categorical association (Cramer's V) — categorical_association
code: df <- data.frame(a = c("x", "x", "y", "y"), b = c("p", "p", "q", "q"),                  c = c("m", "n", "m", "n")) categorical_association(df) #>   a b c #> a 1 1 0 #> b 1 1 0 #> c 0 0 1

what: Compare numeric columns across groups — compare_groups
title: Compare numeric columns across groups — compare_groups
text: Grouped profiling: split data categorical column summarise numeric column within group (count, mean, sd, median, missingness). quickest way see whether metric differs segment.

title: Compare numeric columns across groups — compare_groups
Compare numeric columns across groups — compare_groups
text: df data frame. group Name grouping column. categorical/logical (low-cardinality column); warning issued many levels. max_groups Maximum number groups erroring (guards accidentally grouping near-unique column). Default 50.

title: Compare numeric columns across groups — compare_groups
text: list group_sizes (data frame group, n) numeric_by_group (long data frame group, column, n, n_missing, mean, sd, median), NULL numeric columns compare.

title: Compare numeric columns across groups — compare_groups
code: compare_groups(iris, "Species") #> $group #> [1] "Species" #>  #> $group_sizes #>        group  n #> 1     setosa 50 #> 2 versicolor 50 #> 3  virginica 50 #>  #> $numeric_by_group #>         group       column  n n_missing  mean        sd median #> 1      setosa Sepal.Length 50         0 5.006 0.3524897   5.00 #> 2      setosa  Sepal.Width 50         0 3.428 0.3790644   3.40 #> 3      setosa Petal.Length 50         0 1.462 0.1736640   1.50 #> 4      setosa  Petal.Width 50         0 0.246 0.1053856   0.20 #> 5  versicolor Sepal.Length 50         0 5.936 0.5161711   5.90 #> 6  versicolor  Sepal.Width 50         0 2.770 0.3137983   2.80 #> 7  versicolor Petal.Length 50         0 4.260 0.4699110   4.35 #> 8  versicolor  Petal.Width 50         0 1.326 0.1977527   1.30 #> 9   virginica Sepal.Length 50         0 6.588 0.6358796   6.50 #> 10  virginica  Sepal.Width 50         0 2.974 0.3224966   3.00 #> 11  virginica Petal.Length 50         0 5.552 0.5518947   5.55 #> 12  virginica  Petal.Width 50         0 2.026 0.2746501   2.00 #>

title: Correlation analysis — correlation_analysis
text: Correlation matrices numeric columns, using pairwise-complete observations.

title: Correlation analysis — correlation_analysis
text: df data frame. types Optional named character vector column types. method Character vector; "pearson", "spearman". Default .

title: Correlation analysis — correlation_analysis
text: named list correlation matrices (one per requested method), NULL fewer two numeric columns.

what: dataProfilerR: automated exploratory data analysis — dataProfilerR-package
title: dataProfilerR: automated exploratory data analysis — dataProfilerR-package
text: dataProfilerR profiles data frame single call. infers column types, quantifies missingness, computes distributional statistics, runs normality tests, detects outliers, measures correlation, rolls findings data-quality score. also builds set ggplot2 visualisations. main entry point profile_data(), returns data_profile S3 object print(), summary() plot() methods.

title: dataProfilerR: automated exploratory data analysis — dataProfilerR-package
text: package uses S3 object system. profiling result plain list class "data_profile", keeps structure transparent easy inspect, serialise, extend. S4 add formality (overhead) EDA result object need.

title: dataProfilerR: automated exploratory data analysis — dataProfilerR-package
text: Maintainer: Muhammad Farooqi mqfarooqi@gmail.com Authors: Muhammad Farooqi mqfarooqi@gmail.com

title: Data quality score — data_quality_score
text: Rolls several signals single 0-100 score letter grade. components completeness (share non-missing cells), row uniqueness (penalises duplicate rows), column variability (penalises constant, single-value columns). outlier_rate supplied adds cleanliness component. Components averaged supplied weights.

title: Data quality score — data_quality_score
code: data_quality_score(   df,   missing = NULL,   outlier_rate = NULL,   weights = c(completeness = 0.4, uniqueness = 0.2, variability = 0.2, cleanliness = 0.2) )

title: Data quality score — data_quality_score
text: df data frame. missing Optional result analyze_missing(); computed NULL. outlier_rate Optional fraction (0-1) numeric cells flagged outliers; supplied, cleanliness component included. weights Optional named numeric vector component weights. Missing components dropped rest renormalised.

title: Data quality score — data_quality_score
text: list score (0-100), grade (letter), components (named numeric vector component scores).

what: Detect outliers in a numeric vector — detect_outliers
title: Detect outliers in a numeric vector — detect_outliers
text: Three standard rules: "iqr": outside Q1 - k*IQR / Q3 + k*IQR (Tukey's rule, k = 1.5). "zscore": absolute z-score threshold (default 3). "robust": absolute modified z-score using median MAD threshold (default 3.5); resistant outliers detecting.

title: Detect outliers in a numeric vector — detect_outliers
Detect outliers in a numeric vector — detect_outliers
text: x numeric vector. method One "iqr", "zscore", "robust". threshold Cutoff "zscore"/"robust"; IQR multiplier "iqr". Defaults: 1.5 (iqr), 3 (zscore), 3.5 (robust).

title: Detect outliers in a numeric vector — detect_outliers
text: list: method, n (non-missing count), n_outliers, pct, is_outlier (logical vector aligned x, FALSE NA), bounds (lower/upper, applicable).

title: Detect outliers in a numeric vector — detect_outliers
code: detect_outliers(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 100), method = "iqr") #> $method #> [1] "iqr" #>  #> $n #> [1] 5 #>  #> $n_outliers #> [1] 1 #>  #> $pct #> [1] 20 #>  #> $is_outlier #> [1] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE  TRUE #>  #> $bounds #> lower upper  #>    -1     7  #>

what: Infer a semantic type for each column — infer_column_types
title: Infer a semantic type for each column — infer_column_types
text: Maps column one "numeric", "integer", "date", "logical", "categorical", "text" "". Character columns split "categorical" "text" heuristically: long strings, high-cardinality columns values unique, treated free text; everything else categorical.

title: Infer a semantic type for each column — infer_column_types
Infer a semantic type for each column — infer_column_types
text: df data frame. text_min_avg_chars Average character length character column considered free text. Default 50. text_unique_ratio Fraction unique values character column (enough rows) considered free text. Default 0.8.

title: Infer a semantic type for each column — infer_column_types
text: named character vector inferred types, one per column.

title: Infer a semantic type for each column — infer_column_types
code: infer_column_types(data.frame(a = 1:3, b = c("x", "y", "z"),                               d = Sys.Date() + 0:2)) #>             a             b             d  #>     "integer" "categorical"        "date"

what: Is an object a data_profile? — is_data_profile
title: Is an object a data_profile? — is_data_profile
text: object data_profile?

title: Is an object a data_profile? — is_data_profile
Is an object a data_profile? — is_data_profile
text: x object.

title: Is an object a data_profile? — is_data_profile
text: TRUE x class data_profile.

title: Is an object a data_profile? — is_data_profile
Sample excess kurtosis — kurtosis
text: Moment-based kurtosis minus 3, normal distribution scores near 0.

title: Sample excess kurtosis — kurtosis
text: x numeric vector.

title: Sample excess kurtosis — kurtosis
text: single numeric value, NA_real_ fewer four non-missing values variance zero.

what: Normality tests for numeric columns — normality_tests
title: Normality tests for numeric columns — normality_tests
text: Runs Shapiro-Wilk test numeric/integer column, Anderson-Darling test well suggested nortest package installed. Shapiro-Wilk requires 3 5000 observations; larger columns reduced evenly-spaced subsample 5000. subsample deterministic touch session's random-number state.

title: Normality tests for numeric columns — normality_tests
Normality tests for numeric columns — normality_tests
text: df data frame. types Optional named character vector column types. alpha Significance level normal verdict. Default 0.05.

title: Normality tests for numeric columns — normality_tests
text: data frame one row per numeric column: column, n_used, shapiro_W, shapiro_p, ad_A ad_p (Anderson-Darling columns NA nortest absent), logical normal. Returns NULL numeric columns.

title: Normality tests for numeric columns — normality_tests

what: Outlier summary across numeric columns — outlier_summary
title: Outlier summary across numeric columns — outlier_summary
text: Applies detect_outliers() every numeric column tabulates result.

title: Outlier summary across numeric columns — outlier_summary
Outlier summary across numeric columns — outlier_summary
text: df data frame. types Optional named character vector column types. method Outlier method passed detect_outliers().

title: Outlier summary across numeric columns — outlier_summary
text: list per_column (data frame column, n_outliers, pct) overall_rate (fraction numeric cells flagged, 0-1), NULL numeric columns.

title: Outlier summary across numeric columns — outlier_summary
code: outlier_summary(iris) #> $per_column #>         column n_outliers  pct #> 1 Sepal.Length          0 0.00 #> 2  Sepal.Width          4 2.67 #> 3 Petal.Length          0 0.00 #> 4  Petal.Width          0 0.00 #>  #> $method #> [1] "iqr" #>  #> $overall_rate #> [1] 0.006666667 #>

title: Plot a data profile — plot.data_profile
text: Returns one figures built profile_data().

title: Plot a data profile — plot.data_profile
code: # S3 method for class 'data_profile' plot(   x,   which = c("missing", "correlation", "association", "boxplots", "pairs", "distribution"),   column = NULL,   ... )

title: Plot a data profile — plot.data_profile
text: x data_profile object (built build_plots = TRUE). figure: "missing", "correlation", "association", "boxplots", "pairs", "distribution". column Column name, required = "distribution". ... Ignored.

title: Plot a data profile — plot.data_profile
text: ggplot2 object (also drawn called console).

title: Plot a data profile — plot.data_profile
code: p <- profile_data(iris) # \donttest{ plot(p, which = "missing")  plot(p, which = "distribution", column = "Sepal.Length")  # }

title: Distribution plot for a single column — plot_distribution

title: Distribution plot for a single column — plot_distribution
Distribution plot for a single column — plot_distribution
text: df data frame. column Name column plot. bins Histogram bins numeric columns. Default 30. max_levels Maximum categories show categorical columns. Default 20.

text: ggplot2 object.

title: Print a concise overview of a data profile — print.data_profile
code: print(profile_data(iris)) #> <data_profile> #>   dataset : iris #>   size    : 150 rows x 5 columns #>   types   : categorical=1, numeric=4 #>   missing : 0.0% of cells; 100.0% of rows complete #>   quality : 99.7 / 100 (grade A) #>   use summary() for details and plot() for figures.

title: Profile a data frame — profile_data
text: package's single entry point. runs type inference, missing-value analysis, summary statistics, normality tests, outlier detection, correlation analysis data-quality score, (optionally) builds set ggplot2 visualisations. result data_profile S3 object print(), summary() plot() methods.

title: Profile a data frame — profile_data
code: profile_data(   df,   dataset_name = NULL,   build_plots = TRUE,   distributions = TRUE,   normality = TRUE,   outlier_method = "iqr",   cor_method = c("pearson", "spearman"),   group_by = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Profile a data frame — profile_data
text: df data frame least one row one column unique, non-empty column names. dataset_name Optional label stored metadata; defaults deparsed name df. build_plots Whether build ggplot2 objects. Set FALSE skip plotting wide data. Default TRUE. distributions Whether build per-column distribution plot (eager, heaviest part plotting). Set FALSE wide data use plot_distribution() demand. Ignored build_plots = FALSE. Default TRUE. normality Whether run normality tests. Default TRUE. outlier_method Method passed outlier_summary(): "iqr", "zscore" "robust". Default "iqr". cor_method Correlation methods: "pearson", "spearman". group_by Optional name categorical column. supplied, grouped comparison numeric columns added diagnostics (see compare_groups()). verbose Print progress messages. Default FALSE.

title: Profile a data frame — profile_data
text: object class data_profile: list elements metadata, statistics, diagnostics, plots call.

title: Profile a data frame — profile_data
code: p <- profile_data(iris) p #> <data_profile> #>   dataset : iris #>   size    : 150 rows x 5 columns #>   types   : categorical=1, numeric=4 #>   missing : 0.0% of cells; 100.0% of rows complete #>   quality : 99.7 / 100 (grade A) #>   use summary() for details and plot() for figures. summary(p) #> Data profile for 'iris' (150 x 5), quality 99.7 (A) #>  #> -- numeric summary -- #>        column   n n_missing  mean    sd variance min  q1 median  q3 max iqr #>  Sepal.Length 150         0 5.843 0.828    0.686 4.3 5.1   5.80 6.4 7.9 1.3 #>   Sepal.Width 150         0 3.057 0.436    0.190 2.0 2.8   3.00 3.3 4.4 0.5 #>  Petal.Length 150         0 3.758 1.765    3.116 1.0 1.6   4.35 5.1 6.9 3.5 #>   Petal.Width 150         0 1.199 0.762    0.581 0.1 0.3   1.30 1.8 2.5 1.5 #>  skewness kurtosis #>     0.312   -0.574 #>     0.316    0.181 #>    -0.272   -1.396 #>    -0.102   -1.336 #>  #> -- normality (Shapiro-Wilk) -- #>        column n_used shapiro_p normal #>  Sepal.Length    150  1.02e-02  FALSE #>   Sepal.Width    150  1.01e-01   TRUE #>  Petal.Length    150  7.41e-10  FALSE #>   Petal.Width    150  1.68e-08  FALSE #>  #> -- outliers (iqr) -- #>        column n_outliers  pct #>  Sepal.Length          0 0.00 #>   Sepal.Width          4 2.67 #>  Petal.Length          0 0.00 #>   Petal.Width          0 0.00 #>  #> -- strongest correlations (pearson) -- #>          var1         var2 correlation #>  Petal.Length  Petal.Width       0.963 #>  Sepal.Length Petal.Length       0.872 #>  Sepal.Length  Petal.Width       0.818 #>   Sepal.Width Petal.Length      -0.428 #>   Sepal.Width  Petal.Width      -0.366 #>  Sepal.Length  Sepal.Width      -0.118 #>  # \donttest{ plot(p, which = "correlation")  # }

what: Render a profile to a self-contained HTML report — report
title: Render a profile to a self-contained HTML report — report
text: Turns data_profile standalone HTML file containing metadata, quality score, statistical tables every figure. report built rmarkdown, working pandoc installation required (R Markdown's usual dependency); report() errors clearly pandoc unavailable.

title: Render a profile to a self-contained HTML report — report
code: report(   x,   output_file = "dataProfilerR_report.html",   title = NULL,   quiet = TRUE )

title: Render a profile to a self-contained HTML report — report
text: x data_profile (built build_plots = TRUE). output_file Path write. bare file name lands working directory. Default "dataProfilerR_report.html". title Report title. Defaults dataset name. quiet Passed rmarkdown::render(). Default TRUE.

title: Render a profile to a self-contained HTML report — report
text: path written file, invisibly.

title: Render a profile to a self-contained HTML report — report
code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("rmarkdown", quietly = TRUE) &&     rmarkdown::pandoc_available()) {   p <- profile_data(iris)   f <- report(p, file.path(tempdir(), "iris_report.html")) } #> Report written to /tmp/Rtmpj9rurA/iris_report.html # }

code: summarize_columns(iris) #> $numeric #>         column   n n_missing     mean        sd  variance min  q1 median  q3 #> 1 Sepal.Length 150         0 5.843333 0.8280661 0.6856935 4.3 5.1   5.80 6.4 #> 2  Sepal.Width 150         0 3.057333 0.4358663 0.1899794 2.0 2.8   3.00 3.3 #> 3 Petal.Length 150         0 3.758000 1.7652982 3.1162779 1.0 1.6   4.35 5.1 #> 4  Petal.Width 150         0 1.199333 0.7622377 0.5810063 0.1 0.3   1.30 1.8 #>   max iqr   skewness   kurtosis #> 1 7.9 1.3  0.3117531 -0.5735679 #> 2 4.4 0.5  0.3157671  0.1809763 #> 3 6.9 3.5 -0.2721277 -1.3955359 #> 4 2.5 1.5 -0.1019342 -1.3360674 #>  #> $categorical #> $categorical$Species #> $categorical$Species$n #> [1] 150 #>  #> $categorical$Species$n_missing #> [1] 0 #>  #> $categorical$Species$n_unique #> [1] 3 #>  #> $categorical$Species$top #> [1] "setosa" #>  #> $categorical$Species$top_freq #> [1] 50 #>  #> $categorical$Species$top_pct #> [1] 33.33 #>  #>  #>

code: summary(profile_data(iris)) #> Data profile for 'iris' (150 x 5), quality 99.7 (A) #>  #> -- numeric summary -- #>        column   n n_missing  mean    sd variance min  q1 median  q3 max iqr #>  Sepal.Length 150         0 5.843 0.828    0.686 4.3 5.1   5.80 6.4 7.9 1.3 #>   Sepal.Width 150         0 3.057 0.436    0.190 2.0 2.8   3.00 3.3 4.4 0.5 #>  Petal.Length 150         0 3.758 1.765    3.116 1.0 1.6   4.35 5.1 6.9 3.5 #>   Petal.Width 150         0 1.199 0.762    0.581 0.1 0.3   1.30 1.8 2.5 1.5 #>  skewness kurtosis #>     0.312   -0.574 #>     0.316    0.181 #>    -0.272   -1.396 #>    -0.102   -1.336 #>  #> -- normality (Shapiro-Wilk) -- #>        column n_used shapiro_p normal #>  Sepal.Length    150  1.02e-02  FALSE #>   Sepal.Width    150  1.01e-01   TRUE #>  Petal.Length    150  7.41e-10  FALSE #>   Petal.Width    150  1.68e-08  FALSE #>  #> -- outliers (iqr) -- #>        column n_outliers  pct #>  Sepal.Length          0 0.00 #>   Sepal.Width          4 2.67 #>  Petal.Length          0 0.00 #>   Petal.Width          0 0.00 #>  #> -- strongest correlations (pearson) -- #>          var1         var2 correlation #>  Petal.Length  Petal.Width       0.963 #>  Sepal.Length Petal.Length       0.872 #>  Sepal.Length  Petal.Width       0.818 #>   Sepal.Width Petal.Length      -0.428 #>   Sepal.Width  Petal.Width      -0.366 #>  Sepal.Length  Sepal.Width      -0.118 #>

title: dataProfilerR 0.2.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-24 Changes requested initial CRAN review: Added method references (Shapiro-Wilk, Anderson-Darling, Cramer’s V) Description field. normality_tests() longer touches global random-number state. Large columns now reduced deterministic, evenly-spaced subsample instead set.seed() + sample(); seed argument removed.

title: dataProfilerR 0.2.0
text: New analysis reporting: report() renders complete profile self-contained HTML file (requires pandoc, via ). categorical_association() plot_association() add Cramer’s V categorical columns (categorical analogue correlation matrix). analyze_dates() profiles date/datetime columns: range, unique count, largest gap consecutive timestamps. compare_groups() summarises numeric columns within levels grouping column (grouped/comparative profiling). Pipeline changes: profile_data() gains group_by (adds grouped comparison diagnostics) distributions (set FALSE skip eager per-column distribution plots wide data). Association date results now part returned object, plot() accepts = "association". summary() now also prints date, association grouped-comparison sections present.

title: dataProfilerR 0.1.0
text: First version: profile_data() type inference, missing-value analysis, summary statistics (incl. skewness/kurtosis), normality tests, outlier detection (IQR/z-score/robust), correlation analysis, data-quality score, ggplot2 figures, returned data_profile S3 object print(), summary() plot() methods.
